name: Trolltunga
description: Walk 2,300 feet above the ground on the "Troll's Tongue."

name: Atlantic Road
description: A serpentine highway twists and turns over the treacherous Norwegian Sea.

name: Under
description: The world's largest underwater restaurant is submerged off the southern tip of Norway.

name: Viking Ship Museum
description: Home to three historic ships from the age of Vikings.

name: Urnes Stave Church
description: An intricately carved Viking church overlooks a glistening fjord.

name: Uredd Rest Area (Ureddplassen)
description: Norway has built what may be the world's most beautiful public toilet.

name: Kjeragbolten
description: This giant boulder is pinched over a 1000-meter abyss by two mountains.

name: Fantoft Stave Church
description: The burning of this 12th-century church kicked off a campaign of Norwegian Black Metal terror.

name: Vigeland Sculpture Park
description: One man's exploration of the human form, including the bizarre and delightful "Man Attacked by Babies."

name: Nigardsbreen Ice Cave
description: A stunning silver lining to global warming's gloomy cloud.

name: Lofoten Islands
description: The convergence of two currents makes this a surprisingly pleasant getaway in the Arctic Circle.

name: Preikestolen
description: This "preacher's pulpit" once used for pagan ritual, is home to a new, and dangerous ritual.

name: Flåmsbana (Flåm Railway)
description: One of the most breathtaking railways in Europe also takes one of the steepest climbs, from fjord to mountain plateau.

name: Emanuel Vigeland Mausoleum
description: An artist's mausoleum with a haunting echo.

name: Steinsdalsfossen Waterfall
description: Walk behind a 50-meter-high waterfall.

name: Tubakuba
description: Stay overnight in the world's most sleekly designed rabbit hole, free of charge.

name: Fløibanen Funicular
description: A favorite with sightseers, this Norwegian funicular provides easy access to a stunning view of Bergen and its environs.

name: Sverd I Fjell
description: Monuments don't get much more metal than these three giant viking swords planted in a Nordic hill.

name: Svalbard Global Seed Vault
description: This Arctic storage facility is a secure backup for agricultural biodiversity.

name: Kon-Tiki Museum
description: Across the Pacific in a raft, a museum dedicated to Thor Heyerdahl's now legendary expedition.

name: Giant Sun Mirrors of Rjukan
description: For six months of the year, three giant mirrors direct a pool of sunshine onto this otherwise occluded town.

name: Ægir Brewery
description: A Viking-style brewpub named for a Norse sea giant, in the heart of fjord country.

name: Forest of the Future Library
description: One thousand trees were planted in Norway to be used to print books a century from now.

name: Aurland Lookout
description: A thin pane of glass keeps visitors from sliding off this futuristic skywalk.

name: Red Beach at Mjelle
description: According to the locals, the sands of this secluded Norway beach are infused with gemstone particles.

name: Grass Roots Square
description: Thousands of miniature bronze sculptures have taken over this city square creating a unique art installation.

name: Tromsø Arctic Reindeer Experience
description: At this Arctic outpost, get to know members of the indigenous Sámi community and meet their herds.

name: The Mini Bottle Gallery
description: Tens of thousands of tiny bottles stuffed with various items.

name: Longyearbyen
description: In the northernmost city in the world, it's illegal to be buried because it’s too cold for bodies to decompose.

name: Fram Museum
description: Housing the famous Norwegian ice-defying 19th century polar exploration ship.

name: North Cape
description: At the very tippity-top of Europe is a cliff from which you can view the midnight sun.

name: Edvard Munch's Grave
description: The painter of "The Scream" is laid to rest under a stoic bust of himself in the humble corner of an Oslo cemetery.

name: Polaria
description: The world's northernmost aquarium looks like it has fallen over.

name: Gol Stave Church
description: This handsomely carved wooden church is more than 800 years old.

name: Oslo Opera House
description: The design of this beautifully crafted building was inspired by Norwegian glaciers.

name: 'She Lies'
description: This floating sculpture of ice is a poignant reminder of nature's power.

name: Laerdal Tunnel
description: Longest road tunnel in the world features an air treatment plant and the illusion of daylight.

name: Pyramiden
description: This abandoned mining village was predicted to resist decay longer than any other modern human settlement.

name: Kannesteinen Rock
description: Thousands of years of crashing waves gave this mushroom-shaped rock its svelte figure.

name: Saltstraumen Maelstrom
description: World's strongest whirlpool.

name: Korketrekkeren
description: Oslo's historic toboggan run has been letting thrill seekers slide from train stop to train stop for over a century.

name: Hrimnir Ramen
description: A microbiologist-chef is slinging noodles with hyperlocal Nordic ingredients.

name: Iron Age Farm
description: A reconstructed farm shows what life was like in Norway 1,500 years ago.

name: The Devil of Oslo
description: In downtown Oslo, a menacing and terrifying relief clings to a church wall.

name: Stavanger Cathedral
description: Norway's oldest cathedral still in use.

name: Sorrisniva Igloo Hotel
description: The world's northernmost ice hotel.

name: Neseblod Records
description: This record shop and unofficial black metal museum is an ode to Oslo's heaviest musical export.

name: Svalbard Bryggeri
description: The world's northernmost brewery uses water from a 2,000-year-old glacier to create its beer.

name: Halvikhula
description: The largest cave in Northern Europe.

name: Lepramuseet (Leprosy Museum)
description: St George's Leprosy Museum depicts the gruesome afflictions of those suffering from the disease.

name: Storelgen (The Big Moose)
description: The world's largest moose statue towers above the highway between Oslo and Trondheim.

name: Kistefos Museum
description: The grounds and buildings of an old wood pulp mill have been transformed into an innovative art park and museum.

name: Torghatten
description: A naturally occurring tunnel in this Nordic mountain makes it look like it's been shot.

name: Sti For Øye
description: Art becomes an enchanting part of the environment at this wooded sculpture park.

name: Barentsburg
description: This Arctic mining town is home to a cat that's spent years masquerading as a fox.

name: Geilo Ice Music Festival
description: Norway's chilly all ice-instrument festival.

name: St. Hallvard's Church and Monastery
description: The Brutalist building harbors an unusual inverted dome that swoops down over its central sacred room.

name: Kristiansand Kanonmuseum
description: The last fully functional fortress of Germany's World War II Atlantikwall.

name: Ocean Wave Power Plants
description: Ruins of two abandoned stations destroyed by the power they sought to harness.

name: The Rakke Compass
description: What might look like a cliffside sacrificial altar at first glance is actually a stone compass pointing to cities and lighthouses all around the world.

name: Arctic Cathedral (Ishavskatedralen)
description: This functioning parish church is also the site of many concert performances due to its wonderful acoustics.

name: Hessdalen AMS
description: Research station devoted to mysterious floating lights seen in a Norwegian valley.

name: Minnehallen: The Hall of Remembrance
description: Norway's national monument to fallen seamen.

name: Steilneset Memorial
description: The people murdered during a 17th-century Norwegian witch hunt have been honored with a pair of stark, modern installations.

name: Mølen
description: Mysterious man-made rock piles form an ancient cemetery on this rocky beach in Norway.

name: Z2 Georg Thiele Shipwreck
description: The rusted hull of a WWII German destroyer pokes above the surface in a Norwegian fjord.

name: Arctic Circle Centre
description: This roadside attraction looks like a flying saucer parked atop the tundra.

name: Trondheim CycloCable
description: The world's first and only escalator for lazy bicycles and their daunted riders.

name: Royal and Ancient Polar Bear Society
description: This one-room museum is dedicated to Norwegian history and jokes.

name: Visnes Statue of Liberty
description: A little Lady Liberty stands in the Norway town where the copper for the real thing came from.

name: Edvard Grieg Museum Troldhaugen
description: The former home of Norway's greatest romantic composer.

name: Sandefjord Museum
description: Europe's only museum solely dedicated to the history of whaling still has a fully-functioning whaling ship.

name: Keiko's Memorial Cairn
description: Mound of stones marking the burial site of the world's most famous orca.

name: Bastøy Prison
description: Once the scene of a young boy's home in revolt, this Norwegian island is now home to the world's first "ecological" prison.

name: Heksestein (The Witch Stone)
description: A memorial to 350 people burned at the stake for witchcraft.

name: Diskobukta Kittiwake Colony
description: Thousands of birds nest in the cliffs while arctic foxes prowl below.

name: Noviken VLF Transmitter
description: Large military radio station without transmitting towers.

name: Roald Amundsen Monument
description: The great polar explorer is remembered in this far northern town, where he embarked on his final expedition.

name: Lofoten Krigsminnemuseum (Lofoten War Memorial Museum)
description: A collection documenting Nazi occupation of Norway, including a painting believed to be made by Hitler.

name: Arctic–Alpine Botanic Garden
description: The world's northernmost botanic garden has 25 collections of plants from arctic and alpine regions.

name: Iddis: The Norwegian Printing and The Norwegian Canning Museum
description: A former factory now pays tribute to a local industry fueled by sardines.

name: Mount Halti Peak
description: Citizens of Norway want to gift the peak of Mount Halti to Finland for its 100th birthday.

name: Dornier Do 17M-1 Crash Site
description: The remains of a German plane still sit in the Norwegian wood where it crashed in 1942.

name: Austrått Pyramid
description: This strange pyramid is a unique feature of the Austrått Manor.

name: 'Santa'
description: A bold and controversial statue of a Christmas icon, blending adult undertones with playful humor, stands year-round in a traffic circle.

name: Smeerenburg Blubber Ovens
description: These circular ruins are all that remain of a 17th-century Arctic whaling outpost.

name: Holmenkollen Ski Jump
description: A stunning architectural yet functional feat, towering over Oslo.

name: Hardanger Fartøyvernsenter (Hardanger Maritime Museum)
description: A living museum working to keep the traditions of building and repairing handmade wooden boats, ropemaking, and blacksmithing alive.

name: Norwegian Petroleum Museum
description: An impressive museum dedicated to the resource that made the Scandinavian country rich.

name: 'Untitled'
description: This solitary convex mirror sculpture at an abandoned ferry station at the edge of a fjord reflects the scenery back at the viewer.

name: Vikingodden
description: This one-of-its-kind shipyard brings the 12th-century Viking Age back to life.

name: Svalbard Satellite Station
description: This Norwegian satellite station provides ground services to more satellites than any other facility in the world.

name: 'Myth (Sphinx)—Kate Moss'
description: This striking sculpture of a supermodel in a yoga pose challenges traditional public art.

name: Executed Russian POWs' Memorial Graves
description: Five soviet prisoners of war were executed at this spot just days before the liberation of Norway.

name: Kjærlighetskarusellen (The Carousel of Love)
description: The urinal, known by many names and designated as a Norwegian Cultural Heritage Site for its simple and functional design, stands as a testament to old gay history in Norway.

name: Flørlitrappene
description: These 4,444 wooden steps straight up a mountain were built to serve a hydroelectric power station but these days, they're mostly used by hikers.

name: ‘The Scream’ View
description: The hillside where Edvard Munch experienced the panic attack that inspired “The Scream” offers one of the most striking evening views in Oslo.

name: Marilyn Monroe Statue
description: The iconic blonde bombshell has a familial connection to this town in southern Norway.

name: Capitoline Wolf Statue
description: A statue that would be at home in Rome can instead be found in the heart of Norway's oldest city.

name: 'The Rising Tide'
description: A unique sculpture designed to outline humanity's reliance on oil.

name: Hardanger Skyspace
description: Watch a magnificent, ever-changing colorscape inside James Turrell's immersive work of art.

name: Spåkenes Coastal Fort
description: The gnarled remains of an exploded German bunker from World War II sit along a breathtaking fjord.

name: Rockheim
description: A museum dedicated to Norwegian music, old and new alike.

name: Damstredet & Telthusbakken
description: Charming small streets lined with well preserved wooden houses dating to the 18th and 19th century.

name: Jutulholet
description: Local legends say that a giant who tried to destroy a nearby church lived in this rock cave.

name: Kinnarodden (Cape Nordkinn)
description: Remote and wild, Kinnarodden is the northernmost point of mainland Europe.

name: Troll Museum
description: The first and only troll museum in Norway has interactive exhibits, friendly staff and a small gift shop.

name: Path of Peace
description: An opportunity to walk in the footsteps of Nobel Peace Prize winners.

name: 'Glory Glory with Crutches'
description: Located within military grounds, this one-legged bronze soldier poignantly showcases the human cost of war.

name: Dora I
description: At this concrete bunker on the Trondheim fjord, secrets lie above and below the water's surface.

name: 'Fakkelmannen' ('The Torchbearer')
description: This mountainside mural of a person carrying a torch was created for the 1994 Winter Olympics.

name: Grip Stavkyrkje
description: On a tiny island in northwestern Norway, one of the country's few remaining original stave churches.

name: 'Havmannen'
description: A stoic sculpture that overlooks the waters.

name: Drammen Spiral
description: A marvel of engineering, this road ascends a Norwegian hill through six spirals inside of it.

name: Valberget Utsiktspunkt
description: In a city of wooden houses, this watchtower was vital for early fire detection.

name: Donald Trump Bench
description: This unlikely 1980s relic at a bus station recalls the New York real estate tycoon before his political career.

name: Hulen in Bergen
description: This student-run nightclub was originally a World War II bomb shelter.

name: Hope Cathedral
description: A floating interfaith chapel made from ocean plastic.

name: French Toilets of Spikersuppa
description: A celebration of Norwegian independence, or public art gone wrong?

name: Luftskipsmasta (Airship Tower)
description: Norwegian explorer Roald Amundsen's airship docked here before leading the first-ever flight over the North Pole.

name: Stavanger Astronomical Society Observatory
description: Peer into the night sky with one of Norway's oldest associations of land-based space explorers.

name: 'Broken Column'
description: An art project designed to encourage people to explore and investigate their surroundings more closely

name: Norway’s Largest Stone
description: This 65,000-ton glacial boulder stands as a striking natural monument.

name: Honningsvåg Bamse Statue
description: This beloved wartime mascot has been honored with not just one, but two statues.

name: Norsk Skieventyr
description: This small museum celebrates the father of modern skiing, as well as Norway's other notable contributions to skiing.

name: Nordisk Bibelmuseum (Nordic Bible Museum)
description: Over 4500 valuable bibles are safely tucked away in this museum in central Oslo.

name: The University Observatory (Observatoriet)
description: History, science or architecture fans will love this hidden gem in Oslo.

name: The Bugøy Cannon
description: Commemorating the story of how a small Norwegian village escaped destruction during World War II.

name: Cannonball in Tollbugata
description: A cannonball fired during a war against Sweden is wedged in the wall of this old post office.

name: Nordnes Totem Pole
description: A 10-meter totem pole stands as a tribute to Seattle’s sister city of Bergen, Norway.

name: Stavanger Døvekirke
description: This church conducts its services entirely in Norwegian sign language.

name: Villa Stenersen
description: Take a guided tour at this tucked-away villa, which is an internationally recognized architectural gem.

name: Andrée Strindberg Frænkel Memorial
description: Here ended one of the most ambitious North Pole expeditions in Arctic history.

name: Norwegian Museum of Magic
description: A small collection of magic tricks and memorabilia is now an apartment-sized museum.

name: Siste Skanse
description: The world's most charming instant portrait studio also happens to be its smallest.
